subheadline: See how teams use Desiderio to turn shipped code into customer-ready changelogs, in-app notes, and email digests.

slides.title: Turn shipped work into release notes
slides.lead: Desiderio reads merged pull requests and turns them into customer-ready updates.
slides.description: Review the suggested changelog, adjust the tone, and publish a clean release note without rebuilding the same summary for every channel.

image.title: Release planning session
image.alt: A team discussing tasks at a whiteboard during an agile planning session.
image.credit: Copyright/credit: Photo by blue sky on Unsplash. Used as seeded demo imagery.

slides.title: Publish to every channel at once
slides.lead: One approved update can feed your changelog, product UI, and email digest.
slides.description: Keep message, timing, and links consistent while editors choose exactly where each release update should appear.

image.title: Product publishing dashboard
image.alt: A laptop displaying a dark modern dashboard interface.
image.credit: Copyright/credit: Photo by Neil Fernandez on Unsplash. Used as seeded demo imagery.

slides.title: Keep the same voice every release
slides.lead: Reusable brand rules keep automated drafts from sounding generic.
slides.description: Base every note on your approved examples, glossary, and product language so customer communication stays clear and familiar.

image.title: Editorial workspace
image.alt: A bright modern office with glass walls, teal accents, and clean work areas.
image.credit: Copyright/credit: Photo by Deliberate Directions on Unsplash. Used as seeded demo imagery.
